target.title: fix(openai): allow Codex Spark via harness
target.body:
## Summary
- allow `openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark` when the OpenAI provider is using the Codex harness transport
- keep Spark suppressed for direct OpenAI API / Responses routes where the API catalog rejects it
- add Spark to the Codex fallback catalog and update docs/tests

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior addressed**: `openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark` was blocked globally as "not exposed by Catalog" even when the account's Codex harness catalog exposes it.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux using a real signed-in OpenAI account with Codex harness access. Account identifiers are redacted.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Queried the live Codex app-server catalog, evaluated the current source manifest suppression behavior with the repo `extensions` directory as bundled plugin metadata, then built and restarted the gateway on head `e2def8616b` with the Spark route selected. Latest branch head `f0bcd326d0` is a test-only review-blocker cleanup; gateway build/restart was not rerun after that test-only commit.
- **Evidence after fix**:

```console
$ HOME=/home/clawuser node_modules/.bin/tsx -e '...listAllCodexAppServerModels({ agentDir: "[redacted-agent-dir]" })...'
Config warnings:
- plugins.entries.lossless-claw: plugin disabled (disabled in config) but config is present
{
  "found": true,
  "model": {
    "id": "gpt-5.3-codex-spark",
    "hidden": false,
    "inputModalities": ["text"],
    "supportedReasoningEfforts": ["low", "medium", "high", "xhigh"],
    "defaultReasoningEffort": "high"
  }
}

$ HOME=/home/clawuser OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=/home/clawuser/openclaw/extensions OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 node_modules/.bin/tsx -e '...resolveManifestBuiltInModelSuppression(...)...'
{
  "codexHarness": null,
  "directResponses": {
    "suppress": true,
    "errorMessage": "Unknown model: openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark. gpt-5.3-codex-spark is not exposed by the OpenAI API catalog. Use the Codex harness route when your signed-in account exposes it."
  },
  "directApiHost": {
    "suppress": true,
    "errorMessage": "Unknown model: openai/gpt-5.3-codex-spark. gpt-5.3-codex-spark is not exposed by the OpenAI API catalog. Use the Codex harness route when your signed-in account exposes it."
  }
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The live Codex harness catalog reports Spark as available and non-hidden for the signed-in account; OpenClaw's source suppression rules no longer suppress the Codex harness route (`codexHarness: null`) while direct Responses/API-host routes still suppress Spark. Gateway build/restart proof was submitted at https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88906#issuecomment-4699375181.
- **What was not tested**: Gateway build/restart was not rerun after `f0bcd326d0` because that follow-up only changes tests/test support. Full CI was not rerun locally.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/codex/provider.test.ts extensions/openai/openai-provider.test.ts src/config/config.model-ref-validation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.forward-compat.errors-and-overrides.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-suppression.test.ts src/model-catalog/manifest-planner.test.ts`
- Latest review-fix commit: `f0bcd326d0`; pushed after addressing the June 13 ClawSweeper blockers.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-suppression.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/model.forward-compat.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm run lint:tmp:tsgo-core-boundary`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Live gateway proof remains on pre-review-fix head `e2def8616b`: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/88906#issuecomment-4699375181

Note: full `tsc --noEmit` OOMed at 2GB and 4GB; an 8GB run stayed CPU-active for ~16 minutes with no output and was stopped. A direct `pnpm tsgo:prod` attempt later got stuck in pnpm dependency status/install retrying optional package fetches, so I ran the underlying core tsgo command directly.
target.author: keshavbotagent
